item.title: Zijin Gold Acquires Control of Chinese Rival for $2.6 Billion
item.description: The gold unit of Zijin Mining Group Co. will acquire a controlling stake in Chifeng Jilong Gold Mining Co. for 18.26 billion yuan ($2.64 billion), cementing the group’s status as China’s top miner of the precious metal.

item.title: Thailand Bond Outflow Hits $1 Billion, Set for Largest Since 2022
item.description: Global funds dumped more than $1 billion of Thai bonds this month, putting the market on track for its biggest foreign selloff in four years, as investors exit emerging markets on escalating Middle East tensions.

item.title: Samsung, SK Hynix Leveraged ETFs Set for May Debut, Report Says
item.description: South Korea’s first ever single-stock leveraged exchange‑traded funds — tied to chip bellwethers Samsung Electronics Co. and SK Hynix Inc. — are set to debut as early as May, according to a local media report.

item.title: AI Startup Upstage in Talks to Buy 10,000 AMD Chips in Korea
item.description: Korean AI startup Upstage is in discussions with Advanced Micro Devices Inc. to buy 10,000 of its latest AI accelerators as part of an effort to bring large-scale compute into the country.

item.title: South Korea’s Defense Startups Surge as Global Conflicts Flare
item.description: South Korea’s defense industry has long been one of the country’s most closed corporate ecosystems. Now, startups making experimental weapons for modern warfare are emerging, aided by an uptick in global conflict.

item.title: Emerging-Market Assets Slip After Trump’s Ultimatum to Iran
item.description: Emerging-market assets declined as President Donald Trump’s ultimatum to Iran raised the risk of further disruptions to Middle East energy supplies.

item.title: Surging Rupee Volatility Signals More Pain for Indian Currency, Equity Bulls
item.description: The currency’s sharp slide, rising stock outflows keep investors on edge as market stress builds.

item.title: Goldman Sachs Raises Oil Forecasts on Largest-Ever Supply Shock
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. raised its oil price forecasts for 2026 due to the prolonged disruption of flows through the Strait of Hormuz, which it described as the largest-ever supply shock for global crude markets.

item.title: Majority of Japanese Oppose Sending Warships to Middle East
item.description: A majority of Japanese people oppose sending warships to the Middle East in response to the war in Iran, according to two polls conducted over the weekend amid continued US pressure on allies to help secure the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Sinopec Flags Chemicals Spending Cut as Profit Pressure Mounts
item.description: Sinopec has set a flexible budget target for this year, flagging a potential capital expenditure cut of as much as 20%, after it reported a steeper-than-expected decline in profit for last year.

item.title: Copper Falls to Three-Month Low as Global Growth Concerns Rise
item.description: Copper extended declines to the lowest level in more than three months as the Middle East war sapped risk appetite across financial markets and raised concerns about global inflation and growth.

item.title: Chinese Pig Prices Hit 15-Year Low as War Costs Worsen Margins
item.description: Chinese pig farmers are facing worsening losses, as tepid domestic consumption of pork is compounded by rising costs from the war in Iran.

item.title: BTS Comeback Show’s Turnout Falls Short, Sparking Hybe Selloff
item.description: Hybe Co.’s shares plunged as much as 15% after a heavily-promoted comeback concert by K-pop megastars BTS drew a smaller crowd than authorities initially expected.

item.title: VPBank Seeks $1.2 Billion in One of Vietnam’s Largest ESG Deals
item.description: Vietnam Prosperity JSC Bank is seeking a sustainability‑linked loan of about $1.2 billion, according to people familiar with the matter, in what would be one of the country’s largest financings tied to ESG metrics.

item.title: Indian Insurers Boost State Bond Derivatives as Yields Climb
item.description: Indian insurers are turning to state government bonds for a popular derivatives trade, locking in higher yields amid record provincial debt supply.

item.title: Japan Bond Yields Near Multi-Decade Highs as War Fuels Inflation
item.description: Japan’s government bonds fell on Monday morning, pushing yields back toward multi-decade highs, as concerns mount that the widening conflict in the Middle East will stoke inflation.

item.title: Japan’s FX Head Says Will Take All Possible Steps Anytime
item.description: Japan’s top currency official said the government will take all possible steps to respond to foreign exchange moves as needed, sending a fresh warning to speculators as the yen remains under pressure amid tensions in the Middle East.

item.title: Global LNG Exports Fall to Six-Month Low on Iran Conflict
item.description: Global liquefied natural gas exports declined to a six—month low, erasing recent supply additions from the US and elsewhere as the conflict in the Middle East throttles flows.

item.title: Pakistan Tells Cricket Fans to Stay Home as Fuel Shortages Bite
item.description: Pakistan has ordered fans of its top cricket tournament to stay home and watch matches on television to conserve fuel as disruptions from the Middle East war continue to batter the South Asian nation’s economy.

item.title: Japanese Stocks Slide as Hormuz Fears Amplify Risk-Off Mood
item.description: Japanese stocks dropped after Donald Trump’s threats of attacks on power plants around the Strait of Hormuz exacerbated market concerns over climbing oil prices and fueled risk-off sentiment.

item.title: Australian Stocks Tumble, Eyeing Technical Correction Territory
item.description: Australian stocks fell, nearing a technical correction as investors’ anxieties over the Iran War hit the country’s commodity-rich market.

item.title: South Korea’s Early Exports Show Resilience Despite Iran Turmoil
item.description: South Korea’s export growth maintained momentum in early March, signaling resilient demand even as surging energy prices and renewed trade uncertainty cloud the outlook for global demand.

item.title: Singapore Bonds Ride Out Crude Oil Surge as Other Havens Falter
item.description: Singapore’s bonds have beaten all their developed-market peers this year as the war in Iran has bolstered haven demand, and fund managers say they are still one of the safest places to be.

item.title: Activists Target Japan’s Shipping Stocks on Rising Vessel Prices
item.description: Activist investors are piling into Japanese shipping stocks as limited shipbuilding capacity and elevated freight rates boost the value of their fleets.

item.title: Elliott Said to Take Multibillion-Dollar Stake in Synopsys
item.description: Activist investor Elliott Investment Management has made a multibillion-dollar investment in Synopsys Inc. and plans to push for changes at the chip-design software maker, according to people familiar with the matter who asked not to be identified because it was private.

item.title: Asia’s Busiest Earnings Week Puts Tech, Consumer Shares in Focus
item.description: Asia’s busiest earnings week is offering investors an early test of whether the hype around artificial intelligence is yielding real profits and if consumer demand is showing signs of a more sustainable recovery.

item.title: Oil’s Surge Forces Latin America to Overhaul Its Energy Policies
item.description: Latin American governments are launching a sweeping realignment of energy and fiscal policies, warning that the surge in oil prices from the Iran war threatens regional stability.

item.title: Gold Rises After Worst Week in Four Decades as War Risks Mount
item.description: Gold gained modestly after the biggest weekly drop in more than 40 years, supported by dip-buying as traders weighted the escalating war in the Middle East.

item.title: Oil Fluctuates as Trump’s Hormuz Ultimatum Fails to Stir Traders
item.description: Oil fluctuated after an initial small gain, as investors assessed President Donald Trump’s ultimatum to Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and Tehran’s threat of more reprisals in the Middle East war.

item.title: BTS Details Its Plan to Break to Boy Band Curse
item.description: The biggest boy band in the world returns after a three-year break. Can they recapture the magic?
